english: 1. also ltir-ba (ltir ba), to be distended, inflated, to belly; lto-ldir (lto ldir) a big belly; ltó-ldir-čan (lto ldir can) big-bellied. — 2. to rush, to roar, of the wind W.; to roll, of the thunder, °brag ldir (brag ldir) it thunders; ldir bźin (ldir bzhin) like thunder; ldir - sgra (ldir sgra) a thundering, roaring noise; ldir-čé-ba (ldir che ba) thundering Thgr.

pf. ldugs (ldugs) (Lex.), blugs (blugs) (usual form), fut. blug (blug), imp. blug(s) (blug(s)), col. blug-pa (blug pa), to pour, snód-du (snod du); lág-ču blugs (lag chu blugs) pour some water on my hands, give me water for washing; to sprinkle, to strew, sand Glr.; to cast, to found, metals. Cf. blugs (blugs) and lugs (lugs).

pf., fut. and imp. blud (blud), col. blúd-pa (blud pa), to give to drink, to water, cattle etc., with accus. of the drink given, dug blúd-čiṅ mi °či (dug blud cing mi 'chi) he does not die by a poisoned draught, btúṅ-ba blud (btung ba blud) he gives (him) to drink Thgr.; túg-pa légs-par blúd-čiṅ (thug pa legs par blud cing) making (another) eat plenty of soup Lt., as one also says: túg-pa °túṅ-ba (thug pa 'thung ba) to eat soup.

1. vegetables, greens, in general. — 2. W. lettuce, salad; ldum-nág (ldum nag), a kind of lettuce Cs.; ldúm-bu (ldum bu) 1. Cs. plant, stalked plant. 2. prob. for ldóm-bu (ldom bu) Mil.; 3. C. vulgar pronunciation for sdóṅ-po (sdong po). — ldúm-ra (ldum ra) 1. W. kitchen-garden; 2. fruit-garden, orchard, and 3. esp. flower-garden (better sdúm-ra (sdum ra)); ldúm-ra-pa (ldum ra pa) gardener Pth.
1. for dúm-po (dum po) Glr.; 2. Ld. for zlúm-po (zlum po), round; Mil. also ldúm-la °gril-ba (ldum la 'gril ba) made round, rounded off.
Lexx. miṅ(-gi)-lde (ming (-gi) -lde) w. e.; lde-k'a (lde kha) Sch.: 'belonging together, of the same species'.
Med. 1. Cs. mixture, syrup (?); 2. ointment Wdṅ.
(Sch. also °dé-ba ('de ba)), pf. (b)ldes ((b)ldes), fut. blde (blde), imp. ldes (ldes), to warm one's self, c. accus., me (me), at the fire; nyí-ma (nyi ma), in the sun (not me-la (me la)).
B. and C. (Ts. col. *de-mág* (de mag) Bal. *le-míg* (le mig), otherwise not in use in W.) 1. key, lde-čáb (lde chab) Glr. prob. id. — 2. introduction, preface Cs.
1. Cs. also sdeu (sde'u), a kind of pease, Hind. मुद्रा — 2. v. lde-gu (lde gu).
(pf. bldeg (bldeg)?) to quake, shake, tremble, e. g. of the palace of the gods Dzl.
1. Sch. leaf, sheet, of paper; 2. = ldebs (ldebs) 1.
1. Cs. = ldég-pa (ldeg pa); 2. Sch. to bend round or back, to turn round, to double down.

1. side, Lex. = °dabs ('dabs), e. g. of a mountain Sch., the flat side of a sword or knife Cs.; rús-pai °búr-poi ldebs (rus pa'i 'bur po'i ldebs) by the side of, near, the protuberance of a bone. — 2. compass, enclosure, fence Sch. — 3. C., W. a large cloth, in which a person is carried by several others, either by means of a pole, or by taking hold of the four corners. This mode of conveyance is called Dandi (डण्डी Hindi). — 4. in the Wdṅ. it seems to have still another signification.
1. v. ldém-pa (ldem pa) I. — 2. statue, idolatrous image, idol, standing upright, cf. ldém-pa (ldem pa) II., C. — 3. suspension-bridge (?) Ld.-Glr. Schl. 17, a; v. ldém-pa (ldem pa) III.

I. sbst. 1. Cs.: 'contrariety, opposition, irony', which seems not to be quite inconsistent with the explanation given by Zam., draṅ-mín (drang min), as being an intentional concealing of the true sentiment. — ldém(-po) (ldem (-po)) riddle, enigma (cf. tsód-bya (tshod bya)); mi-ldem (mi ldem), byá-ldem (bya ldem), bém-ldem (bem ldem) an enigma or allegory applied to men, to birds, to inanimate beings; ldém-poi ṅag (ldem po'i ngag), ldém-gtam (ldem gtam) parable, allegory; ldem-dgóṅs (ldem dgongs) Lex. = Ssk. अभिसंधि, prob.: a concealed deceitful intention, Sch.: 'a mysterious opinion'; ldém-rjód-pa (ldem rjod pa) Cs. to say a riddle or parable, *ldem ṭad-cè* (ldem thad ce) W. to propose a riddle, ldem tsód-pa (ldem tshod pa) Cs., čód-pa (chod pa) Sch., to solve a riddle. — 2. W. a trap (C. *pur-nyi* (pur nyi)), *bi-ldém* (bi ldem)
